summary: Excellent audience recording that captures a high‑energy Telluride show with standout performances of Scarlet Begonias, Uncle John’s Band/Big River and Eyes of the World.
review: The Aug 15‑16 1987 Telluride shows are praised for their intense vocals, crisp playing and memorable moments – especially the scorching Scarlet Begonias, a killer Uncle John’s > Big River segue and deep renditions of Eyes of the World. Reviewers repeatedly note that this is one of the best audience tapes from ’87, with clear, top‑notch sound despite a few comments about occasional mud or long Franklin’s Tower intros.

ai_rating.rationale: The majority of reviews (10/12) are 4‑5 stars and highlight strong musical moments; recording quality is repeatedly called “top notch” or “best audience recording,” outweighing the single lower‑rated comment about modest sound.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Many reviewers call it the best ’87 audience tape – crisp, clear and well balanced. One reviewer notes the quality isn’t perfect but overall the mix is solid with minimal dropout.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set described as a bit slow (notably an extended Franklin’s Tower), but the second set erupts with high energy, tight transitions and powerful vocal performances.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for strong, intense playing – especially on Scarlet Begonias and the Fire sequence.
band_member_comments.Phil: Highlighted as “so strong” in several reviews; his bass work is noted as a driving force throughout the shows.
